programmes.show_description: Roofer Simon has fallen two storeys onto concrete, Evelyn's burnt her finger on a cheese stick, and 97-year-old Doris is in resus with a fast heart rate.
A car fanatic unloads some of his beloved - and very valuable - classic car collection to raise retirement funds. And could a rare animal pelt keep a cash-strapped innkeeper afloat? (Ep3/3)
Davide: In Boston, Gordon Ramsay visits Davide, an Italian restaurant that's been run into the ground by brothers Frank and Anthony. Can they learn to trust each other again?
From a teen's suburban bedroom to the heart of Silicon Valley, the story of a heartbroken father's bid for the truth behind his daughter's death, caught up in a powerful algorithmic spiral.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: On the Isle of Wight, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien help Helen and her husband Dave to restore a metal garden bench and an old water feature.
In Gloucestershire, there's a malfunctioning blender, a singing dog, and plenty of catty comments, as Debbie and Paul, Angela and John, and Kirsty and James compete.
...The Final: The finalists create mini model theatres and tackle a tricky throwing technique. Whose final curtain call will crown them as champion?
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Colin Jackson in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
The Beer Is Always Greener: While Cheers is being redecorated, Norm, Cliff and the regulars are seduced by another bar. And is Woody and Kelly's marriage on the rocks?
The King of Beers: Norm can't believe his luck when he's offered a job as a beer taster in a brewery. Rebecca gets hooked on playing a new slot machine.
The Magnificent Six: Henri challenges Sam to a contest to find out who's the biggest ladies' man. Rebecca aims to quit smoking. Woody and Kelly try to rekindle their honeymoon.
Neil Simpson - and his guide Rob Poth - go in the Men's Giant Slalom. They took home a bronze in this event from the 2023 World Championships. Can they do even better here in Cortina?
Can ParalympicsGB's Scott Meenagh medal in the Para Biathlon Sprint Pursuit? This is Scott's third and final Games. What a story if he could take a podium spot. Plus: Mixed Team Curling.
Teenager Dom Allen from Fife makes his ParalympicsGB debut alongside his sporting heroes in the Standing Alpine Skiing Giant Slalom. Can he make his mark on the Paralympic world stage?
Channel 4's midday news summary.
...Highlights: Shanghai International Circuit hosts the first Sprint of 2026, as the teams look to snaffle the early-season points. Can George Russell double up after his win in Melbourne?
Laura and Nick dream of making a permanent move to Estepona on Spain's Costa del Sol with their beloved dog Poppy. Can Jean Johansson find their perfect place for their £160,000 budget?
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Colin Jackson in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Italy, can Donna keep up as holiday reservations fly in? And in France, will unseasonable weather put a dampener on Paul and Melissa's big summer event?
The new run continues. On payment day, a number of B&Bers are certain that their competitors have been doing some tit-for-tat marking. When the dust settles, who'll win the week?
The new run continues. In Manchester, Danny takes his guests on a culinary journey from Spain to Thailand. Will his night be anything to write home about? And who'll bag the £1000? (Ep5/5)
Beijing hero Neil Simpson goes in the Giant Slalom. And can Scott Meenagh end his Paralympics career on a high in the Para Biathlon Sprint Pursuit? Plus: Mixed Team Curling.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
The Conservative Party: Helen Whately MP, Shadow Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, sets out the Conservative Party's plan to get more people into work.
...Luxurious Cruises: A look behind the scenes aboard some of the world's most opulent cruise ships, where suites cost up to £8k a night and pets are as pampered as their people.
The Capture, Married at First Sight Australia and Louis Theroux: Inside the Manosphere are some of the shows on this time. And there's also Will and Ralf Should Know Better.
Richard Ayoade and Michelle Wolf join Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker to pick over the week's news and the latest action from the Milano Cortina Winter Paralympics.

programmes.show_name: Greg Davies: The Back of My...
programmes.show_description: ...Mum's Head: Greg Davies' acclaimed live comedy. Stung by his mum's suggestion that he's not normal, Greg puts everyone under the microscope in a quest to discover if anyone actually is.
...to the Orchestra: Let Bill be your guide as he joins forces with maestro Anne Dudley and the BBC Concert Orchestra for a humorous fusion of music and comedy at the Royal Albert Hall.
Cafe Tavolini: In Bridgeport, Connecticut, Gordon Ramsay visits Cafe Tavolini, a restaurant that's being neglected as the couple who run it teeter on the edge of divorce.
Alan Johnson and his sidekick Dave Owen host at Dunsandles Guest House in New Brighton on Merseyside. Things get off to a hairy start with some scandalous sheets.
Nick and Gina Mead welcome their rivals to the Lady Teal Hotel Boat on the waterways near Burnley, where the afternoon entertainment is a freezing competition out on the locks.
Brock hopes that his sense of humour and perfectionist nature will impress his guests at York House in Tetbury, Gloucestershire. But his reluctance to go off-menu at breakfast disappoints.
Hospitality veterans Leo and Sonya Brooke-Little host at Heyford House in Oxfordshire. One guest settles some scores during an afternoon of boxing.
The B&Bers meet for one last time to settle some scores and find out how much they've been paid. After accusations of over-enthusiastic hosting, the winner is announced.
Taking inspiration from Coventry Cathedral, Keith and Marj create a Victorian-style parlour bathroom, complete with a giant candlestick holder and Marj's dream lampshade.
In Essex, there are deep-fried jam sandwiches and some attention-grabbing burlesque, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
Swim Neighbors: Carrie refuses to have a shared garden with the neighbours, so they spite her by building a swimming pool, which proves too tempting for Doug...
S'No Job: Uncle Stu asks Doug to get his son Danny a job at IPS, but Doug doesn't want to work with his cousin. Arthur takes part in film audience research.
The Incredible Lightness of Being a Baby: Cletus becomes a helium tycoon. Maggie reunites with baby Hudson from Playdate with Destiny.
Bart the Bad Guy: When Bart sees the superhero movie of the summer before anyone else, he uses his 'spoiler powers' to blackmail the town.
Can Menna Fitzpatrick replicate her golden glory of 2018 in the first run of the Slalom? And there's multiple ParalympicsGB interest in the Men's and Women's Snowboard Banked Slalom.
ParalympicsGB's James Barnes-Miller, Nina Sparks, Davy Zyw and Hester Poole all go in the Snowboard Banked Slalom. Plus: Menna Fitzpatrick goes for gold in Run 2 of the Slalom.
As the Para Cross Country, Snowboarding and Alpine Skiing Slalom action concludes, the excitement builds for the start of the Mixed Team Wheelchair Curling gold medal match.

programmes.show_name: F1 Chinese GP Sprint Race and...
programmes.show_description: ...Qualifying Highlights: An eventful Qualifying session in Melbourne saw Mercedes lock out the front row, with George Russell on pole. Can the Silver Arrows carry the momentum to Shanghai?
Dabi and Devi can't wait to swap the grey skies of Croydon for the sunshine and heat in the seaside town of Gandia in Valencia. Lee Juggurnauth shows them what their £180k budget can buy.
Phil catches up with Chloe, who wanted to fly the nest to be near buzzy Caversham, and Ella and Grant, who needed something spectacular to tempt Grant out of his mum's house.
A lifeboat is converted into a camping pod. A mum and three daughters create a charming straw bale house. And George enjoys an oriental delight on the Isle of Wight.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Ade Adepitan has all the best action, with a veritable dump of skiing and snowboarding medals on the slopes. Plus: enjoy the slide at the Wheelchair Curling Mixed Team gold medal match.

programmes.show_name: New: The Race for Ancient Egypt...
programmes.show_description: ...in Colour: In 1922 the discovery of Tutankhamun's tomb sparks global mania, while Egypt fights for independence, provoking a fascinating struggle for control of the ancient past. (Ep1/2)

programmes.show_name: New: Roman Empire by Train with...
programmes.show_description: ...Alice Roberts: In Nimes, Alice keeps running into crocodiles and Roman landmarks better than any outside of Rome itself. Then she explores Narbonne and hops on a train to Spain. (Ep5)
Bill leaves the bustle of the city behind and seeks solace in the beauty of mountainous Da Lat. With his head in the clouds, Bill finds love in unexpected places. (Ep3)
(2021) Tom McCarthy's crime thriller starring Matt Damon as an all-American, still-waters-run-deep oil worker from Oklahoma who travels to Marseilles to try to get his daughter out of jail.
